title: Fix lookup-table join fan-out — duplicate lookup_name rows and fact_epi_score iso3 join inflate fact tables and spend
description: Two related fan-out bugs in silver-to-gold2 silently duplicate fact rows. (A) gold_dim_country_lookup is built as self-lookups for every dim_country row UNION alias rows, with no dedupe on lookup_name. The alias seed table contains 'Tier 1' self-mappings — ('United States of America','United States of America',1.00) and ('United Kingdom','United Kingdom',1.00) — so when the dim's standard name equals the alias (near-certain for 'United Kingdom' from EPI 2024), the lookup holds two rows with the same lookup_name. fact_procurement and fact_supply_share join on lookup_name, so every matching row is emitted twice and SUM(fact_procurement[spend_eur]) — the headline DAX measure — doubles for affected countries. (B) fact_epi_score joins the lookup on iso3 instead of lookup_name; the lookup intentionally has one row PER ALIAS sharing the same iso3, so EPI scores duplicate once per alias (2-7x for alias-rich countries: US, UK, Turkey, DRC, Koreas), violating the documented grain 'one row per country x indicator x year' (gold_tables.md:35).
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the redundant Tier 1 self-mapping rows from country_aliases_with_confidence (lines 145-147) OR make the redundancy harmless: gold_dim_country_lookup and gold_dim_material_lookup are deduplicated on lookup_name before write (e.g. dropDuplicates(['lookup_name']) with highest-confidence row kept via window/orderBy — arbitrary dedupe is not enough, the exact-match row must win).

fact_epi_score no longer joins dim_country_lu on iso3 (line 978). Replace with a deduplicated iso3 -> country_key map (e.g. dim_country select iso3, country_key, dropDuplicates(['iso3'])) or join gold_dim_country directly.
After /run-gold on Fabric: SELECT lookup_name, COUNT(*) FROM gold_dim_country_lookup GROUP BY lookup_name HAVING COUNT(*)>1 returns 0 rows (same for material lookup).
After /run-gold: fact_epi_score has exactly one row per (country_key, indicator_key, year) — verify with a GROUP BY ... HAVING COUNT(*)>1 returning 0 rows.

Spend is not inflated by fan-out, measured numerically: gold SUM(fact_procurement[spend_eur]) reconciles against silver SUM(quantity * unitpriceeur). RESCOPED 2026-07-22 from a report-side before/after comparison of UK- and US-headquartered suppliers — that comparison is unsatisfiable (silver_procurement contains no UK-headquartered suppliers at all, and no pre-fix snapshot was taken), and the direct silver-vs-gold reconciliation is stronger evidence of the same property.

No fan-out from the country/material lookup joins, measured on the invariant that survives the territory rollup: SUM(fact_supply_share.share_pct) equals the source share sum over valid-stage rows (fan-out inflates it; a rollup merge leaves it unchanged). Rowcount is a secondary cross-check — fact_supply_share row count equals silver_globalsupplyshares row count minus dropped invalid-stage rows minus the territory-rollup merge count printed by silver-to-gold; any rowcount difference NOT explained by the rollup is a defect.
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED (pipeline quality audit 2026-07-13): findings ranked #1 and #2 (critical). Not spec-decomposed — empty drift fingerprints, like task-022.

EVIDENCE (A): tier-1 self-alias rows at silver-to-gold2 notebook-content.py:145-147; lookup built without dedupe at lines 469-490 (self-lookups UNION alias-join); fact_procurement joins hq/prod country on lookup_name at lines 1093-1094; fact_supply_share joins country on lookup_name at line 1222 and material at 1218-1219. No uniqueness check on lookup_name exists anywhere (data_quality_checks checks duplicates on bronze only).

EVIDENCE (B): fact_epi_score join at line 978: epi_long.join(dim_country_lu, on=epi_long.iso == dim_country_lu.iso3, how='left'). dim_country_lu = gold_dim_country_lookup, which holds one row per alias per country (same iso3 repeated).

MATERIAL LOOKUP: the same self-mapping hazard applies structurally to gold_dim_material_lookup (lines 715-738) though no current alias equals a standard name; dedupe it anyway so future alias additions can't reintroduce the bug.

INTERACTION with task-025: dim_country currently has duplicate iso3 rows (Turkey) resolved nondeterministically — fixing that changes which standard names exist and therefore which alias rows survive the inner join. Same file; do 025 in the same session or immediately after, and verify the acceptance queries after BOTH fixes.

DOWNSTREAM: DAX Total Spend EUR = SUM(fact_procurement[spend_eur]) (dax_measure_library.md:123) and HHI/concentration measures over fact_supply_share[share_pct] are the consumers being corrupted. The semantic model reads these tables via DirectLake (fabric/OEMInsightBI_v2.SemanticModel/definition/tables/).
GUARD: consider adding an assert-style check in the notebook right after the lookup writes (fail loudly if lookup_name not unique) — cheap insurance independent of task-026's DQ wiring.

CLAUDE HALF DONE 2026-07-14 (builds on task-025): (A) both gold_dim_country_lookup and gold_dim_material_lookup deduped on lookup_name via row_number() over partitionBy('lookup_name') orderBy(match_confidence desc, match_type=='exact' bucket, key asc) BEFORE write — deterministic, exact self-lookup wins the Tier-1 tie (self-lookups carry match_type='exact'; alias seed uses 'exact_match'/'standard_alias' so never ties on the literal 'exact'). Kept the redundant Tier-1 self-mapping rows in the seed as harmless docs. (B) fact_epi_score now joins dim_country_iso3_map = gold_dim_country.select('iso3','country_key').dropDuplicates(['iso3']) instead of the per-alias lookup on iso3 -> restores 'one row per country x indicator x year'. (GUARD) assert-style lookup_name-uniqueness checks added after both lookup writes. Did NOT touch task-025's dim_country build. py_compile passes. Expected post-fix: US 7->6 unique lookup_names, UK 6->5, Turkey unchanged at 7 (no alias == 'Turkey').

ERIK (Fabric) — remaining acceptance (run /run-gold, then): (crit 3) GROUP BY lookup_name HAVING COUNT(*)>1 = 0 on both lookups (in-notebook asserts also enforce); (crit 4) fact_epi_score GROUP BY (country_key,indicator_key,year) HAVING COUNT(*)>1 = 0; (crit 5) report Total Spend EUR before/after for UK-/US-HQ suppliers -> expect a DROP where fan-out occurred, sanity-check vs silver SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur); (crit 6) fact_supply_share rowcount == silver_globalsupplyshares (minus dropped invalid-stage rows).

VERIFIER NOTE: fact_epi_score reads silver_epi2024results without dropDuplicates(['iso']); if crit-4 query returns rows post-run, the culprit is duplicate source iso rows (orthogonal, pre-existing), not the alias fan-out this fixes. EPI 2024 is normally one-row-per-country so expected clean. Re-run task-023 + task-025 acceptance queries together after BOTH land (per task notes).

2026-07-22 live run: grain_uniqueness@fact_supply_share FAILED (blocking). Before treating this as unfinished fan-out work, read task-026's FINDING 2 — the likely cause is the UNKNOWN-key collapse at silver-to-gold2:1437-1444 plus the constant year literal at :1119, not alias fan-out. Crit 6 (fact_supply_share rowcount == silver_globalsupplyshares minus dropped invalid-stage rows) is now measurable: silver-to-gold Succeeded at 6:44 PM and the Global source is live (2561 rows, was the EU file's 907).

ROOT CAUSE OF THE LAST DUPLICATE, measured 2026-07-22 20:37. The failing grain is (Antimony, P, China, 2023) with shares [0.3, 51.8]. Source rows: China 51.8% (t=1.1) and Hong Kong 0.3% (t=1.0). The alias at silver-to-gold2:188 — ("Hong Kong","China",0.85,"territory") — DELIBERATELY folds the territory into its parent, so both rows land on one grain. Not a bug: a rollup that maps N source rows onto one country is structurally incompatible with a 0%-tolerance uniqueness check on a grain containing country_key. Only two territory aliases exist (the other is French Guiana->France, which does not currently collide); Taiwan is deliberately NOT aliased.

DECISION NEEDED (Erik). Option 1 — aggregate on rollup: SUM share_pct when rows collapse to one grain, so China/Antimony/P becomes 52.1%. Semantically right (if HK is China for this analysis, its supply is China's) and makes the grain unique. BUT the trade parameter differs — China t=1.1 (export-restricted), Hong Kong t=1.0 (baseline) — so the merged row needs a defined t, which is a methodology question for DEC-001/task-038's trade-weighted HHI, not an implementation detail. Option 2 — drop the Hong Kong alias and let HK stand alone; arguably more accurate for supply risk since HK at 0.3% of antimony PROCESSING is likely re-export/trade-hub activity rather than real capacity, but it needs its own dim row and abandons the jurisdiction-risk argument for folding it in. Option 3 — demote grain_uniqueness to advisory; not recommended, it hides genuine future fan-out.

OPTION 1 IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-22 (Erik's choice). Added a territory-rollup aggregation to silver-to-gold2 immediately before write_tbl(fact_supply_share_final): groupBy the full grain and SUM share_pct, so China/Antimony/P becomes 52.1%. Non-key columns combine deliberately — data_quality_score MIN (a merged row is only as trustworthy as its weakest constituent, so the 0.85 territory alias correctly drags it down), quality_category paired to that MIN via struct-min, has_unmapped_* MAX (boolean OR), unmapped_impact_score SUM (share-scaled, so it adds like share_pct), source_row_id MIN (deterministic representative; the per-row audit trail builds separately from fact_supply_share_raw so nothing is lost). Output schema is IDENTICAL to before — 11 columns, same order — so no semantic-model drift.

Added an assert on grain uniqueness immediately after the rollup. If a future alias introduces a new collision the notebook now fails with the offending grains named, rather than the pipeline failing later at grain_uniqueness with a message that does not say which rows collided.

CRIT 6 RESCOPED 2026-07-22 (same session as the rollup). The original wording — 'fact_supply_share row count equals silver_globalsupplyshares row count minus dropped invalid-stage rows' — became WRONG when Option 1 landed: the territory rollup deliberately merges Hong Kong into China, so the counts MUST differ by the merge count, and the criterion as written would fail on correct behaviour. Replaced the primary test with share_pct sum preservation, which is invariant under the rollup (a merge sums two shares into one row, leaving the total unchanged) but still detects fan-out (a duplicated join row inflates the total). Rowcount is retained as a secondary cross-check with the rollup count subtracted. This is a measurement-definition change only — no code change, and the underlying property being asserted ('no fan-out') is unchanged.

task-038 DEPENDENCY recorded in the code comment: t is currently dropped at bronze-to-silver:127, so there is no trade parameter to reconcile. When task-038 carries t through, this aggregation MUST define how t combines — the colliding rows differ (China t=1.1 export-restricted vs Hong Kong t=1.0 baseline) and that is a DEC-001 methodology decision, not an implementation detail.

ALL CRITERIA MEASURED PASS 2026-07-22 on the 20:58 local (18:58 UTC) pipeline run — the first fully green end-to-end run. Acceptance pack at .claude/support/workspace/acceptance-queries-023-025-026.py. Results: (crit 3) gold_dim_country_lookup and gold_dim_material_lookup both 0 duplicate lookup_name; (crit 4) fact_epi_score 0 duplicated (country_key, indicator_key, year) grains, and 0 duplicate iso rows in silver_epi2024results so the orthogonal source-duplication cause the verifier flagged is also ruled out; (crit 5, rescoped) silver SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur) 3,273,776.03 vs gold SUM(spend_eur) 3,273,776.03 — delta 0.00 EXACTLY; (crit 6, rescoped) share_pct sum source 12,412.30 vs fact 12,412.30, delta 0.0000, and rowcount silver 2561 - 0 invalid-stage = 2561 valid vs fact 2560, difference exactly 1 = the single Hong Kong->China territory merge, with distinct mapped source grains 2560 confirming the same number independently.

CRIT 5 BONUS FINDING (feeds task-030 / FR-006): the spend reconciliation is EXACT, not approximate. FR-006 predicted a nonzero delta because gold kg-normalises quantity while leaving unitprice unconverted, so the check was classified advisory in task-026. A 0.00 delta means unit normalisation is currently a no-op on this data (all units effectively uniform), which is evidence that the aggregate_reconciliation check can be promoted from advisory to BLOCKING earlier than task-026's note assumes. Confirm units are genuinely all-kg under task-030 before promoting — a 0.00 delta today does not prove a future mixed-unit load would reconcile.

RE-MEASURED UNCHANGED 2026-07-22 after the alias/commodity seed fixes deployed (commit 5610c35, 19:53 UTC run): crits 3, 4 and 6a all still PASS with identical figures — share sum 12,412.30 both sides delta 0.0000, rowcount 2561 valid vs 2560 fact difference 1, spend 3,273,776.03 both sides. Confirms the seed edits (Russia alias direction, steel alias targets, three commodity-group keys) did not perturb any property this task asserts, which was the risk of touching the shared lookup machinery after closing the task.

NO UK SUPPLIERS EXIST (measured 2026-07-22): silver_procurement carries 13 distinct country spellings across HQ and production roles — China 36, Chile 36, Sweden/Germany/France/Netherlands/Singapore 24 each, Malaysia/DRC/Mexico/'DRC (HQ in South Africa)'/USA/Canada 12 each (counts are HQ+production unioned; fact_procurement is 132 rows). United Kingdom appears nowhere, which is why crit 5's UK half was unsatisfiable rather than failing. USA resolves correctly to 'United States of America' (12 rows, 85,597.93 EUR).

task_verification.notes: RUNTIME HALF COMPLETED 2026-07-22 (runtime_validation partial -> pass). All four Fabric-side acceptance queries measured on the 20:58 local / 18:58 UTC green run: lookup_name unique on both lookups (0 dups), fact_epi_score grain clean (0 dups, and 0 duplicate source iso so the orthogonal cause is excluded), spend reconciles EXACTLY (3,273,776.03 both sides, delta 0.00), share_pct sum preserved (12,412.30 both sides, delta 0.0000) with the rowcount difference of 1 fully explained by the Hong Kong->China territory merge. Crits 5 and 6 were rescoped before measurement — 6 because the territory rollup made the original rowcount equality wrong-by-construction, 5 because no UK-headquartered suppliers exist to compare. Original attempt-1 notes follow. Claude code-half verified (owner:both). Both lookups deduped on lookup_name via row_number() BEFORE write — deterministic + total winner (match_confidence desc, exact-bucket, key asc); exact self-lookup provably beats Tier-1 alias. NO fact-side distinct()/dropDuplicates added -> root-cause fix, not symptom-hide. fact_epi_score repointed to dim_country_iso3_map (deduped iso3->country_key); grain restored, no column ambiguity. Guard asserts present + correct. task-025 dim_country build untouched. py_compile passes. runtime_validation=partial: 4 GROUP BY/spend/rowcount acceptance queries are Erik's Fabric half; code makes all achievable.

verification_history.summary: Root-cause dedupe (not symptom-hide); deterministic winner; epi iso3-map join fix; guards present; py_compile OK. Fabric queries = Erik.

Runtime half of attempt 1 closed (not a new attempt): all Fabric acceptance queries measured green on the 18:58 UTC run. Crits 5 + 6 rescoped first — 6 was wrong-by-construction after the territory rollup, 5 unsatisfiable (no UK suppliers). runtime_validation partial -> pass.

test_protocol.summary: Fabric-side confirmation of task-023 (Erik runs /run-gold in Fabric UI)

steps.instruction: SELECT lookup_name, COUNT(*) FROM gold_dim_country_lookup GROUP BY lookup_name HAVING COUNT(*)>1
SELECT country_key, indicator_key, year, COUNT(*) FROM fact_epi_score GROUP BY 1,2,3 HAVING COUNT(*)>1
steps.expected: 0 rows (one row per country x indicator x year)

steps.instruction: Compare Total Spend EUR in the report before/after for UK- and US-HQ suppliers
steps.expected: Corrected value drops where fan-out occurred; sanity-check vs silver SUM(quantity*unitpriceeur)

steps.instruction: SUM(share_pct) in fact_supply_share vs the source share sum over valid-stage rows; then rowcount as a cross-check
steps.expected: Share sums equal (delta < 0.01) — no fan-out. Rowcount difference equals exactly the territory-rollup merge count printed by silver-to-gold; anything else is a defect

test_protocol.automated_results: Static verification passed (py_compile OK; dedupe totality, join fix, guards, scope all confirmed by code trace). Live Spark/Fabric execution not runnable locally.
